item.title: U.S. venture investors are backing fewer Canadian deals, bucking a years-long trend
item.description: The post U.S. venture investors are backing fewer Canadian deals, bucking a years-long trend appeared first on The Logic .

item.title: Donald Trump starts putting his new tariff wall together
item.description: The administration is preparing to impose Section 301 duties supposedly against unfair trade Alan Beattie

item.title: Meta shares rise after report it plans to cut 20% or more of its work force
item.description: Planned layoffs, which would represent the steepest cuts in years, to offset heavy AI spending

item.title: Et tu, S&P 500?
item.description: The SpaceX IPO gamesmanship is going to be epic

item.title: Fed expected to hold rates steady as Middle East war muddies economic outlook
item.description: Central bank is likely to strike a cautious tone when it meets this week as high oil prices stoke inflation concerns

item.title: Wall St underestimates private capital problems, says top credit hedge fund
item.description: Davidson Kempner’s Tony Yoseloff warns a substantial portion of PE firms are already “stressed or distressed”

item.title: BCE plans to build Saskatchewan AI data centre
item.description: Telecom is seeking to diversify revenue streams; facility to cost $1.7-billion

item.title: Canada’s inflation rate eases to 1.8% in February from base-year effect of sales tax break
item.description: CPI reading will factor into this week’s Bank of Canada interest rate decision

item.title: Business Brief: Five files to follow this week
item.description: Oil prices expose vulnerability and opportunity for Canada

item.title: Thames Water creditors offer £6.55bn in new debt to take formal control
item.description: Deal requires agreement with regulators and government and could take a number of months to conclude

item.title: FirstFT: Donald Trump warns European allies on Nato over Hormuz plea
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: wealthy individuals pull $10bn from private credit funds and a good night for One Battle After Another

item.title: Canada, U.S., Britain team up to tackle crypto scams
item.description: Law enforcement agencies launch co-ordinated effort to identify fraud victims, perpetrators

item.title: Non-alcoholic beer and pet grooming added to UK inflation basket
item.description: ONS updates list of items used to measure price growth on back of changing spending habits

item.title: UK should avoid using subsidies to cap energy costs
item.description: Amid Iran war price shock, Labour should propose a better measure than the Tories’ ‘energy price guarantee’

item.title: Stock Markets Today: U.S. futures rise on tech gains as Middle East conflict remains in focus
item.description: Oil rises as focus returns to threats on Middle East export facilities

item.title: Emirates reroutes flights after drone attack on Dubai’s airport
item.description: Flights from Manchester, Edinburgh and Dublin among those that returned to destinations on Monday

item.title: Yale padlock maker to axe new CEO before he starts
item.description: Fortune Brands staves off proxy battle with asset manager set up by Trian co-founder Ed Garden

item.title: In Iran oil shock, Canada talks big but is useless to our allies
item.description: When friendly countries are clamouring for energy, resource-rich Canada can offer nothing

item.title: Fixing Canada’s dysfunctional mortgage system
item.description: In Canada’s mortgage system, the needs of homeowners often clash with the needs of the banks. There’s a better way

item.title: UniCredit launches €35bn Commerzbank takeover offer
item.description: Italian lender steps up long-running pursuit with plan to increase its near 30% stake in German bank

item.title: 2022 redux?
item.description: War makes it possible.

item.title: Submit your questions to Katie Martin: are markets heading for a shock?
item.description: Take part in a live Ask an Expert Q&A with the FT’s markets columnist on Thursday March 19 at 1pm GMT

item.title: The UK is struggling to achieve its aims on clean energy
item.description: Our economy remains highly fossil fuel-dependent partly because we are not electrifying in the desired way Martin Wolf

item.title: Was Revolut’s banking licence worth the wait?
item.description: Group would be wise to move gradually with its lending ambitions

item.title: Canadian pension funds count cost of private equity slump
item.description: Rising interest rates bring prolonged downturn to the buyout industry

item.title: Retail investors pull billions from private capital’s credit gold mine
item.description: Flood of redemptions threatens to stall one of Wall Street’s most important sources of growth

item.title: Private credit is key to keep Main Street moving
item.description: Despite recent concerns, it helps sustain job creation and economic growth

item.title: India’s restaurants stop deep frying food as gas crisis bites
item.description: South Asia reels from global energy crisis sparked by the US-Israeli war on Iran

item.title: Energy groups press UK to boost gas storage after Iran war price shock
item.description: Middle East conflict renews focus on Britain’s resilience to extreme events

item.title: Syngenta to build $120mn agricultural science centre in UK
item.description: Investment by Swiss-based group contrasts with pharma companies withdrawing funding from British research facilities

item.title: Activist plots London market return 16 years after dealmaking ban
item.description: Brian Myerson wants to raise up to £1bn for new defence venture to be listed in the UK

item.title: Singapore warns of illicit funds in popular low-tax investment vehicles
item.description: Product launched by city-state to lure investors from offshore tax havens could be used for money laundering, says regulator

item.title: US shale producers not yet tempted by $100 oil
item.description: Independent operators are caught between Trump’s Iran war aims and his promise of low petrol prices

item.title: Oil slips as Trump calls on countries to help secure Strait of Hormuz
item.description: U.S. President said Sunday he is demanding that other countries help protect the vital energy gateway

item.title: Starmer says UK will not be drawn into ‘wider war’
item.description: Comment from British prime minister comes as German government warns of danger of ‘never-ending’ conflict

item.title: MPs and peers call for major review of UK financial regulation
item.description: Report warns of ‘systemic flaws’ but consumer groups dismiss ‘rehash’ findings

item.title: Canadian Paralympic Committee CEO calls for added investment after dip in medal standings
item.description: Canada finished sixth in total medals (15), after having 25 four years ago in Beijing

item.title: Homeowners with stronger credit scores are increasingly defaulting on mortgage payments
item.description: Rate of ‘near-prime’ borrowers missing payments for 90 days increased by 31 per cent by end of 2025 from year earlier

item.title: Bank of Canada expected to hold rates amid oil-driven inflation risk
item.description: Central bank may strike more hawkish tone as energy prices surge

item.title: Trouble is brewing among America’s corporate borrowers
item.description: Surging energy prices will add to fears of a wave of defaults

item.title: Poilievre unveils new auto plan in bid for tariff-free access to U.S. market
item.description: Plan would remove GST from Canadian-made vehicles, end EV subsidies and ban use of Chinese or Russian-connected software

item.title: Canada’s unemployment rate rises, IEA’s record oil stockpile release and Carney’s Northern major projects: Must-read business and investing stories
item.description: Plus, Canada’s vehicle exports suffered a historic collapse in January

item.title: The cost of digital disorganization is nearly a month of productivity per year
item.description: The problem often stems from unclear systems and ownership inside organizations, but simple habits and clearer workflows can help workers regain focus and reduce wasted time

item.title: Iran war should prompt a North Sea rethink
item.description: Sweating the UK’s natural gas assets can support the country’s green transition

item.title: Trump demands ‘about seven’ countries join coalition to help police Strait of Hormuz
item.description: U.S. President’s earlier appeal to protect vital oil route gets cautious response from allies

item.title: Zelenskyy challenges Europe over Russian oil imports
item.description: Ukrainian president asks if countries ‘collectively decided’ to restart flows from Russia

item.title: Wealth taxes’ design flaws consign them to failure
item.description: It may be easier for countries sitting on large deficits to tighten the screws on existing levies Tax

item.title: Renewable jet fuel may face price war with China, warns top producer
item.description: Neste chief calls for a ‘level playing field’ following heavy investment

item.title: Ontario government restricts new rental buildings in favour of Sanofi pharma plant
item.description: Minister’s Zoning Order limits developer or any future owner to equivalent of 10 stories at proposed site in north Toronto

item.title: Housing affordability has been improving. It’s not enough and may not last much longer
item.description: The conflict in Iran and the prospect of rising interest rates could bring higher borrowing costs

item.title: Even if the Iran war is won, the economy is already lost
item.description: With oil and gas supplies constrained and global shipping snarled, consumers face rising costs and declining living standards

item.title: Is your Isa strategy up to date?
item.description: Upcoming changes to pensions rules will have a significant impact on how people use Isas in retirement Tax

item.title: Vladimir Putin enjoys a huge windfall from the Iran war
item.description: But the sugar high may not last

item.title: Ottawa to allow more hiring of low-wage temporary foreign workers in rural areas
item.description: Announcement comes during tough period for job seekers, driven by economic uncertainty posed by U.S. President Donald Trump’s tariffs

item.title: Lotus plans to be among first to sell Chinese-made EVs in Canada under new quota program
item.description: Lotus Eletre SUV is made in Wuhan, China, and produces up to 905 horsepower for a price tag that will be well into six figures

item.title: Xanadu wants to bring large-scale quantum computer manufacturing to Canada
item.description: The post Xanadu wants to bring large-scale quantum computer manufacturing to Canada appeared first on The Logic .

item.title: Canada’s manufacturing sales fall 3% in January as auto sector slips
item.description: Motor vehicle sales dropped to their lowest level since 2021

item.title: Canada sheds 84,000 jobs in February, unemployment rate rises to 6.7%
item.description: Economy saw a significant drop in full-time jobs as uncertainty weighs on the labour market

item.title: U.S. economy grew at sluggish 0.7% rate in fourth quarter, well below estimate
item.description: Forty-three-day government shutdown hobbled growth in GDP and federal government spending and investment

item.title: U.S. consumer spending and core PCE inflation rise slightly in January
item.description: In the 12 months through January, Personal Consumption Expenditures inflation advanced 2.8%
